evals.name: whole-doc-sqlite
evals.prompt: Apply the documentation grammar rules to example-1.md to make it more readable. Edit the file in place.
evals.expected_output: example-1.md rewritten in place: long sentences split one-idea-per-line, em-dash interruptions become full stops, the parenthetical DB helper list and the stateless clarification moved to section footnotes. Every fact preserved. Close in spirit to example-1-human.md.

evals.name: whole-doc-http
evals.prompt: Run the grammar rules over example-2.md and clean it up in place.
evals.expected_output: example-2.md rewritten in place: sentences split, the three call-site lists and the pre-strict-envelope chain moved to footnotes, restated/announce filler cut, facts preserved. Close in spirit to example-2-human.md.

evals.name: section-only-accounting
evals.prompt: In example-3.md, rewrite only the SP3 section (the one about /api/accounting/get and Declined quotes) using the grammar rules. Leave the SP1 and SP2 sections exactly as they are.
evals.expected_output: Only the SP3 section is rewritten in place. SP1 and SP2 are byte-for-byte unchanged. SP3 facts preserved, asides moved to footnotes within that section.

evals.name: git-diff-added-prose
evals.prompt: I just added a new section to docs/cache.md in this repo. Apply the documentation grammar rules to what I added, but don't touch the prose that was already there.
evals.expected_output: Only the added (uncommitted) section is rewritten in place. The pre-existing committed prose is left unchanged. Skill uses git diff to find the added lines.
